---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: e41e9972a4dd958c3fe078e593210be522d0bbd1
findings:
  - { id: judged-cf-1, kind: judged, text: "Parent dc-5 requires each directory entry whose branch has an open MR to be chipped in-review from the forge port, with an unreachable forge yielding a disclosed absence per entry — but ref-index dc-1 declares ComputeIndex a pure function of ref state and dc-3 closes the Entry field set ({Ref, Source, StatusGroup, SpecStatus, Disclosed}) with no carrier for forge-derived in-review state or a forge-absence disclosure. Since dc-1 also names directory-home as the sole consumer that turns Entry values into markup and claims the output type is designed so that story needs no further plumbing, the second (forge) source dc-5 mandates has no declared home: either ref-index silently drops a parent-required per-entry signal or directory-home must grow its own forge integration, and neither an exempts edge (\"forge chip is out of ref-index scope\") nor a supersedes edge was declared against dc-5. (confidence 0.60)", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory }
  - { id: judged-cf-2, kind: judged, text: "Parent dc-5 says each entry is 'disclosed by source' and a remote-only branch 'renders sealed with its remoteness disclosed', while ref-index dc-3 sets Disclosed to nil for ordinary entries and reserves the shared disclosure.Disclosure shape for degraded cases only (no-draft-spec etc.), treating Source as a bare enum. A remote-only entry is ordinary under dc-3 (Disclosed=nil), so its parent-mandated remoteness disclosure must be fabricated by directory-home outside the shared disclosure vocabulary — contradicting dc-3's own stated rationale that every disclosure renders in the one shared vocabulary, with no declared edge resolving whether remoteness is a disclosure or mere enum-derived presentation. (confidence 0.40)", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory }
  - { id: judged-cf-3, kind: judged, text: "Parent dc-5 admits remote design branches to the enumeration as 'remote-tracking design refs' generally, but ref-index dc-2 narrows the new plumbing to a for-each-ref query scoped to refs/remotes/origin/design/* — hardcoding the 'origin' remote. A repo whose design remote is named anything else silently enumerates as local-only, an undeclared narrowing of dc-5's enumeration contract with no exempts edge recording the single-remote assumption. (confidence 0.30)", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/ref-index#dc-1, spec/ref-index#dc-2, spec/ref-index#dc-3, spec/ref-index#dc-4, spec/workbench-directory#dc-1, spec/workbench-directory#dc-2, spec/workbench-directory#dc-3, spec/workbench-directory#dc-4, spec/workbench-directory#dc-5] }
digest: sha256:3300b19e468810937d00060215a8edf5704e8d86e301ec4c68f1c07250cce538
integrity: sha256:0d5d02d6510dfe3d72f0df96f2d3535c9fe79ca70baa2ffddc695141c9380283
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"cf-1\",\"text\":\"Parent dc-5 requires each directory entry whose branch has an open MR to be chipped in-review from the forge port, with an unreachable forge yielding a disclosed absence per entry — but ref-index dc-1 declares ComputeIndex a pure function of ref state and dc-3 closes the Entry field set ({Ref, Source, StatusGroup, SpecStatus, Disclosed}) with no carrier for forge-derived in-review state or a forge-absence disclosure. Since dc-1 also names directory-home as the sole consumer that turns Entry values into markup and claims the output type is designed so that story needs no further plumbing, the second (forge) source dc-5 mandates has no declared home: either ref-index silently drops a parent-required per-entry signal or directory-home must grow its own forge integration, and neither an exempts edge (\\\"forge chip is out of ref-index scope\\\") nor a supersedes edge was declared against dc-5.\",\"confidence\":0.6,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-directory\"},{\"id\":\"cf-2\",\"text\":\"Parent dc-5 says each entry is 'disclosed by source' and a remote-only branch 'renders sealed with its remoteness disclosed', while ref-index dc-3 sets Disclosed to nil for ordinary entries and reserves the shared disclosure.Disclosure shape for degraded cases only (no-draft-spec etc.), treating Source as a bare enum. A remote-only entry is ordinary under dc-3 (Disclosed=nil), so its parent-mandated remoteness disclosure must be fabricated by directory-home outside the shared disclosure vocabulary — contradicting dc-3's own stated rationale that every disclosure renders in the one shared vocabulary, with no declared edge resolving whether remoteness is a disclosure or mere enum-derived presentation.\",\"confidence\":0.4,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-directory\"},{\"id\":\"cf-3\",\"text\":\"Parent dc-5 admits remote design branches to the enumeration as 'remote-tracking design refs' generally, but ref-index dc-2 narrows the new plumbing to a for-each-ref query scoped to refs/remotes/origin/design/* — hardcoding the 'origin' remote. A repo whose design remote is named anything else silently enumerates as local-only, an undeclared narrowing of dc-5's enumeration contract with no exempts edge recording the single-remote assumption.\",\"confidence\":0.3,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-directory\"}]}" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v1, inputs: [spec/ref-index@e41e9972a4dd958c3fe078e593210be522d0bbd1], digest: sha256:3300b19e468810937d00060215a8edf5704e8d86e301ec4c68f1c07250cce538, integrity: sha256:0d5d02d6510dfe3d72f0df96f2d3535c9fe79ca70baa2ffddc695141c9380283 }
---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-cf-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Parent dc-5 requires each directory entry whose branch has an open MR to be chipped in-review from the forge port, with an unreachable forge yielding a disclosed absence per entry — but ref-index dc-1 declares ComputeIndex a pure function of ref state and dc-3 closes the Entry field set ({Ref, Source, StatusGroup, SpecStatus, Disclosed}) with no carrier for forge-derived in-review state or a forge-absence disclosure. Since dc-1 also names directory-home as the sole consumer that turns Entry values into markup and claims the output type is designed so that story needs no further plumbing, the second (forge) source dc-5 mandates has no declared home: either ref-index silently drops a parent-required per-entry signal or directory-home must grow its own forge integration, and neither an exempts edge ("forge chip is out of ref-index scope") nor a supersedes edge was declared against dc-5. (confidence 0.60)
- **judged-cf-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Parent dc-5 says each entry is 'disclosed by source' and a remote-only branch 'renders sealed with its remoteness disclosed', while ref-index dc-3 sets Disclosed to nil for ordinary entries and reserves the shared disclosure.Disclosure shape for degraded cases only (no-draft-spec etc.), treating Source as a bare enum. A remote-only entry is ordinary under dc-3 (Disclosed=nil), so its parent-mandated remoteness disclosure must be fabricated by directory-home outside the shared disclosure vocabulary — contradicting dc-3's own stated rationale that every disclosure renders in the one shared vocabulary, with no declared edge resolving whether remoteness is a disclosure or mere enum-derived presentation. (confidence 0.40)
- **judged-cf-3** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Parent dc-5 admits remote design branches to the enumeration as 'remote-tracking design refs' generally, but ref-index dc-2 narrows the new plumbing to a for-each-ref query scoped to refs/remotes/origin/design/* — hardcoding the 'origin' remote. A repo whose design remote is named anything else silently enumerates as local-only, an undeclared narrowing of dc-5's enumeration contract with no exempts edge recording the single-remote assumption. (confidence 0.30)
